arts.ttl: Genoa-Juventus match to be played behind closed doors
arts.sum: The anticipated Serie A clash between Genoa and Juventus will be played without spectators, as officially confirmed by authorities. This decision comes in the wake of violent incidents involving some Genoa supporters before and after the Coppa Italia derby on Wednesday evening. The Prefect of Genoa, following recommendations from the Observatory on Sporting Events, has ordered that the match scheduled for Saturday evening at 18:00 at the Luigi Ferraris Stadium be held behind closed doors. The closure means that the stands of the Marassi stadium will be empty for a fixture that was almost certain to have been a sell-out. The post Genoa-Juventus match to be played behind closed doors appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Juventus manager Motta: “I’ll tell you the starting XI for the Genoa game….”
arts.sum: Juventus manager Thiago Motta addressed the media ahead of his team’s match against Genoa, expressing confidence in their progress despite three consecutive 0-0 draws. Motta began by emphasizing his passion for coaching: “I love what I do. I always want to try something different to minimize mistakes. The offensive phase isn’t just about one player, but the entire team. We’re on the right path: we took an important step against Napoli. We maintained possession, took the ball away from them, and that was crucial. Now we need to improve on how we approach the opponent’s penalty area.” Regarding player availability, The post Juventus manager Motta: “I’ll tell you the starting XI for the Genoa game….” appeared first on FootItalia.com.
